100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 5, 1926, a significant political crisis unfolded in Britain as Prime Minister Stanley Baldwin faced mounting pressure from labor unions amid ongoing strikes, raising fears of unrest. Meanwhile, in Berlin, tensions escalated as the German government took steps to address hyperinflation, a growing concern for citizens and the economy. Additionally, the United States continued to grapple with Prohibition, as debates over its effectiveness intensified across the nation.
